vision.block1.title: Think before building
vision.block1.subtitle: Every system responds to a need. Understanding that transformation is the first step before automating, scaling, or deploying.
vision.block2.title: Understand before intervening
vision.block2.toptext1: When I face a complex system, the first thing I try to understand is not the tool or technology, but the reason for existence. Every system responds to a need and transformation of energy (resources, information, or decisions) until producing a result.
vision.block2.toptext2: I try to observe it from a top-down perspective: understanding how that transformation flows, how its parts interact, and at what point the balance can be broken. Without that perspective, any intervention is partial.
vision.block2.bottomtext: Only after understanding the whole does it make sense to optimize a piece.
vision.block3.title: Design for the future state
vision.block3.toptext1: I don't design systems just to solve the present. I think about how they could evolve: what will happen when they grow, when conditions change, or when complexity increases.
vision.block3.toptext2: It has always come naturally to me to imagine the final state of a system before building it. Visualizing its complete architecture, its compatibilities, its limits, and its possible expansion.
vision.block3.bullet5: Ability to adapt to new requirements
vision.block3.bottomtext: Scaling is not adding more resources. It's having designed from the beginning to be able to grow without breaking the balance.
vision.block4.title: DevOps as architecture, not as a tool
vision.block4.toptext1: For me, DevOps is not a collection of tools, but a way of designing system architecture meant to be operable, automatable, and scalable from the first moment.
vision.block4.toptext2: I don't separate development, infrastructure, and operation. I see them as layers of the same architecture that must work as a coherent whole.
vision.block4.bullet2: Infrastructure treated as part of the design, not as an afterthought
vision.block4.bullet3: Automation integrated into the system lifecycle
vision.block4.bullet4: Observability as an architectural property, not as a patch
vision.block4.bullet5: Scalability thought from the initial design
vision.block4.bottomtext: I'm not only interested in a system working. I'm interested in its architecture allowing it to evolve without friction, grow without improvisation, and remain stable under pressure.
vision.block5.title: Where I want to evolve
vision.block5.text1: My goal is to grow towards system architecture design, where technical planning and global vision are part of the process from the start.
vision.block5.text2: I am aware that this evolution requires experience, technical depth, and exposure to real systems. That's why I'm intentionally building that foundation: understanding infrastructure, automation, and operation from within.
vision.block5.text3: I seek environments where I can contribute systemic thinking while continuing to develop my technical capacity. Teams where designing, executing, and learning are part of the same cycle.
vision.block5.text4: I don't just want to maintain systems. I want to understand them, improve them, and learn to design them better each time.
